purpose: Current known deviations from the enforced business boundary. New occurrences must fail verify:business-boundary unless this list is intentionally updated with a reason and nextAction.

deviations.reason: Admin Vue still uses fixed local admin actor and local merchant filters before formal Admin and department login screens are implemented.
deviations.nextAction: Replace fixed adminActorUserId and local merchant IDs with JWT user and scoped identity in the auth entrypoints slice.

deviations.reason: Admin tests still assert fixed local admin and merchant IDs for current local-only flows.
deviations.nextAction: Rewrite tests around logged-in Admin identity and business scopes when Admin login is introduced.

deviations.reason: Admin client still carries refund/payment channel strings and local token fallback before full business identity enforcement.
deviations.nextAction: Constrain payment channels to wechat/alipay and use authenticated Admin identity in the payment realignment and auth slices.

deviations.reason: API auth includes local development users for current Docker verification.
deviations.nextAction: Move from local fixed identities to seeded role-based Admin, Franchise, Merchant, and User accounts.

deviations.reason: Local seed still creates fixed review merchant/franchise/product IDs for Docker smoke flows.
deviations.nextAction: Keep only deterministic seed data while wiring formal login and role contexts to those seeded subjects.

deviations.reason: Order compatibility reads still expose pickupStoreName.
deviations.nextAction: Add merchant address snapshots and keep pickupStoreName read-only compatibility.

deviations.reason: Checkout persistence still stores pickupStoreName compatibility data while sales-franchise and fulfillment-merchant snapshots are being introduced.
deviations.nextAction: Remove pickupStoreName from checkout persistence after merchant address snapshots replace pickup compatibility.

deviations.reason: Checkout validation still requires pickupStoreName for pickup compatibility.
deviations.nextAction: Validate pickup against merchant address instead of pickupStoreName.

deviations.reason: Fulfillment tasks still copy pickupStoreName compatibility data.
deviations.nextAction: Use merchant address snapshots for fulfillment display and pickup verification.

deviations.reason: Payment persistence still stores one channel for the payment rather than explicit welfare-card plus online-cash components.
deviations.nextAction: Introduce combination payment components tied to welfare-card ledger and wechat/alipay cash channel.

deviations.reason: Order reads still expose pickupStoreName compatibility data while returning sales-franchise and fulfillment-merchant snapshots.
deviations.nextAction: Remove pickupStoreName from buyer/Admin reads after merchant address display is wired through every frontend.

deviations.reason: Order API still accepts pickupStoreName and legacy channel values for current local flows.
deviations.nextAction: Move pickup to merchant address and payment to combination components.

deviations.reason: Merchant Vue still uses fixed local merchant and actor IDs before Merchant login.
deviations.nextAction: Read merchantId, franchiseId, and actor user from JWT identity.

deviations.reason: Merchant tests still assert fixed local merchant IDs and pickupStoreName compatibility.
deviations.nextAction: Rewrite tests around logged-in Merchant identity and merchant address display.

deviations.reason: Merchant client still accepts explicit merchant IDs and uses localStorage token fallback.
deviations.nextAction: Use JWT identity as the source of merchant scope and remove explicit conflicting merchant IDs from commands.

deviations.reason: Portal still uses fixed buyer ID, local pickup payload, and merchant wording before buyer login and merchant address snapshots are implemented.
deviations.nextAction: Use logged-in buyer identity, welfare-card account selection, sales franchise display, fulfillment merchant, and merchant address.

deviations.reason: Portal tests still assert fixed buyer ID and compatibility pickup data.
deviations.nextAction: Rewrite tests for authenticated buyer, franchise sales party, merchant address, and combination payment.

deviations.reason: Portal API client still sends welfareCardPaymentAmount as a numeric field and carries pickupStoreName compatibility.
deviations.nextAction: Send selected welfare-card account plus online cash channel component.

deviations.reason: Mini program detail flow still sends numeric welfareCardPaymentAmount and fixed local buyer data.
deviations.nextAction: Use logged-in user identity and selected welfare-card account.

deviations.reason: Mini program detail tests still cover the numeric welfare-card amount shortcut.
deviations.nextAction: Test selected card account, issuer franchise validation, and online cash remainder.

deviations.reason: Mini program order detail still formats compatibility pickup facts and uses local buyer order detail flow.
deviations.nextAction: Show sales franchise, fulfillment merchant, merchant address, and payment components.

deviations.reason: Mini program order detail tests still include pickupStoreName compatibility and cash-channel negative regression fixtures.
deviations.nextAction: Rewrite around merchant address and combination payment display.

deviations.reason: Mini program order list still formats compatibility payment and pickup summaries.
deviations.nextAction: Show sales franchise and normalized payment component summary.

deviations.reason: Mini program order-list tests still use fixed local buyer and old payment text.
deviations.nextAction: Rewrite around authenticated buyer and combination payment display.

deviations.reason: Mini program API tests still assert legacy checkout/payment payload shapes.
deviations.nextAction: Update helpers after card-account and online-cash component API contracts are introduced.

deviations.reason: Checkout utility tests still assert numeric welfareCardPaymentAmount.
deviations.nextAction: Test welfare-card account selection and issuer-franchise validation inputs.

deviations.reason: Order utility still formats pickupStoreName compatibility data.
deviations.nextAction: Format sales franchise, fulfillment merchant, merchant address, and payment components.

deviations.reason: Order utility tests still use pickupStoreName compatibility data.
deviations.nextAction: Rewrite around merchant address and combination payment components.

deviations.reason: Payment utility tests intentionally use cash as a negative fixture to assert it is not accepted as a user payment channel.
deviations.nextAction: Remove the cash fixture once the component payment contract no longer needs legacy-channel regression coverage.

deviations.reason: Refund utility tests intentionally use cash as a negative fixture to assert it is not accepted as a user refund channel.
deviations.nextAction: Remove the cash fixture once the component refund contract no longer needs legacy-channel regression coverage.
